title: Oxytropis davisii

This family is further divided into several subfamilies, with Oxytropis davisii belonging to the subfamily Faboideae. The genus Oxytropis is a group of plants that are native to the mountainous regions of Asia and North America, and are characterized by their pea-like flowers and taprooted habit. Within the genus Oxytropis, Oxytropis davisii is a specific species that is native to the mountainous regions of western North America. The classification of Oxytropis davisii reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants and its unique characteristics.
